differentials.name: Cardiac angiosarcoma
differentials.raw: Cardiac angiosarcoma (involving large areas of heart, more aggressive with penetration of valves + great vessels, central necrosis, prominent enhancement)

differentials.name: Path:  multiple firm nodules; contiguous invasion of pericardium
differentials.raw: Path:  multiple firm nodules; contiguous invasion of pericardium

differentials.name: chest pain; unresponsive rapidly progressive heart failure
differentials.raw: chest pain; unresponsive rapidly progressive heart failure

differentials.name: cardiac tamponade, SVC syndrome Location:   RA > RV > LV > LA > atrial septum > ventricular septum; > 1 chamber (75%) When infiltration beyond the myocardium occurs, the right atrium is most commonly involved, with subsequent venous extension leading to SVC / IVC thrombosis.
differentials.raw: cardiac tamponade, SVC syndrome Location:   RA > RV > LV > LA > atrial septum > ventricular septum; > 1 chamber (75%) When infiltration beyond the myocardium occurs, the right atrium is most commonly involved, with subsequent venous extension leading to SVC / IVC thrombosis.

context: PET: √  difficult to differentiate from normal myocardial uptake DDx: Secondary Cardiac Lymphoma Prevalence:   in 16-28% on autopsy for NHL; pericardial involvement more frequent than for primary; more common in immunocompromised patients Secondary cardiac involvement by lymphoma, either through direct extension or via hematogenous spread, is more common than primary cardiac lymphoma. Primary Cardiac Lymphoma =  lymphoma that involves only heart ± pericardium at time of diagnosis (extremely rare) Histo:  typically NHL: well-differentiated B-cell lymphoma, follicular center cell lymphoma, diffu
